snippet.title: Dobarro afirma que nueva licencia de la OFAC ayudará en la recuperación de la economía
snippet.description:

snippet.title: Страна терпит поражение. Техники нет. Какое 9 мая? Россияне об отмене Парада техники в Москве. Опрос
snippet.description:
Военная техника не будет участвовать в параде Победы в Москве в связи с «текущей оперативной обстановкой»

Военный парад на Красной площади 9 мая пройдет без колонны военной техники. Об этом сообщили в Министерство обороны Российской Федерации.

В параде, приуроченном к годовщине Победы, будет задействована только пешая колонна. Авиационная часть при этом сохраняется — самолеты пролетят над Красной площадью.

Также в этом году в мероприятии не примут участие воспитанники суворовских и нахимовского училищ, а также кадетские корпуса. В Минобороны пояснили, что изменения связаны с «текущей оперативной обстановкой».

Также в этом году в мероприятии не примут участие воспитанники суворовских и нахимовского училищ, а также кадетские корпуса. В Минобороны пояснили, что изменения связаны с «текущей оперативной обстановкой».

snippet.title: Simple Off Grid Radio Secret That Lets You Talk 100 Miles With No Power
snippet.description:
#OffGridCommunication #HamRadio #SurvivalPrepping

When the grid goes down for good, your phone, your router, and every communication system you trust becomes useless — but there's one technology that keeps working across 100+ miles with no power infrastructure whatsoever. It's older than the internet, proven in every major disaster on record, and almost nobody in the prepping community is talking about it the right way.

Amateur radio — specifically HF radio and skywave propagation — is the single most overlooked survival communication tool available to ordinary civilians, and it requires nothing more than a license, a simple antenna, and a handful of batteries.

In this video, we explore:
📡 How HF amateur radio lets you communicate 100+ miles with zero grid power or cell towers
⚡ The exact order in which communication systems collapse during a major disaster
🌐 The science of skywave propagation and why the ionosphere is your best friend when SHTF
🔋 How to power a functional radio setup using nothing but salvaged flashlight batteries
🗺️ Why FM and cell-based communications fail first — and what actually survives long-term grid collapse
🛡️ What military and emergency operators have known for decades that most preppers completely ignore

If you are into survival skills, disaster preparedness, military history, or real-world prepping - you are going to love this breakdown.

Subscribe for more survival documentaries, forgotten history, and real-world prepping knowledge.

snippet.title: Before you take advice from anyone calling themselves an HVAC professional understand what their cre
snippet.description:
Before you take advice from anyone calling themselves an HVAC professional understand what their credentials actually mean.

Florida does not require HVAC technicians to hold a state license. The guy giving you advice may have nothing but a 20 minute online test behind him. Know who you’re taking advice from.

Before you let anyone touch your system ask them what license they hold. In Florida you can look it up in 60 seconds on the DBPR website.

snippet.title: Stirbt der Amateurfunk aus?
snippet.description:
Stirbt der Amateurfunk aus?

In diesem Funk Essay geht es um eine Frage, die viele Funkamateure beschäftigt: War früher wirklich mehr los auf z.B. auf 2 Meter, oder verändert sich der Amateurfunk einfach nur? Oder stirbt er sogar? Ich nehme euch mit auf eine persönliche und nachdenkliche Reise zwischen Relaisfunk, Nostalgie, moderner Funktechnik und der Frage, was den Amateurfunk im Kern eigentlich ausmacht.

snippet.title: Property Tax News: दिल्ली के व्यापारियों की बल्ले-बल्ले, अब प्रॉपर्टी टैक्स के साथ...
snippet.description:
दिल्ली में व्यापार करने वालों के लिए बड़ी राहत की खबर आई है. नगर निगम ने जनरल ट्रेड लाइसेंस और स्टोरेज लाइसेंस की प्रक्रिया को आसान बनाते हुए इसे प्रॉपर्टी टैक्स से जोड़ने का फैसला लिया है. अब व्यापारियों को अलग से लाइसेंस के लिए आवेदन नहीं करना पड़ेगा और एक ही पोर्टल पर टैक्स के साथ लाइसेंस फीस जमा की जा सकेगी. नगर निगम ने तय किया है कि जनरल ट्रेड या स्टोरेज लाइसेंस शुल्क प्रॉपर्टी टैक्स का 15 प्रतिशत होगा. यह राशि हर साल टैक्स के साथ जमा करनी होगी. पहले जहां लाइसेंस बनवाने में हजारों रुपये खर्च होते थे, अब यह खर्च काफी कम हो जाएगा
#MCD #tradelicense #propertytax #Delhimarket #MCDtax #KhabarFastHaryana

snippet.title: The Story Behind POTA with Mike Case W8MSC
snippet.description:
Mike Case W8MSC is one of the key figures behind Parks on the Air, helping transform a one-year, ARRL-sponsored park activation event into a lasting global program.

Mike’s path into ham radio started later than most. Licensed in 2014 after a chance encounter reignited his technical curiosity, he brought a background in IT and a love for the outdoors. Field Day was the spark, but it wasn’t enough. He wanted more than a once-a-year experience. That search led him to National Parks on the Air (NPOTA) in 2016, and when that program sunset, Mike did not step away. Alongside Jason Johnston W3AAX, Thom Martin W8TAM, and others, he helped shape what came next. From a small Michigan-based effort to a national, and now international, platform, he was there building the backend, refining the rules, and testing ideas in real time.

What began as a scrappy operation running on a server in a basement, built with a “maybe we’ll get 1,000 users” mindset, quickly took off. Mike and the team had to evolve fast, moving from a simple LAMP stack to a cloud-based microservices architecture. Along the way came unexpected challenges, from broken log formats to scaling pains and the complexity of global expansion. There is also a human side to it, with activators sharing tips, a small Facebook group turning into a worldwide community, and a program that has become a gateway into the hobby for thousands.

Now POTA is entering its next chapter. With the formation of a formal board, Mike is focused on transferring institutional knowledge and helping set a durable foundation for the future. A new IT system is on the horizon, with faster awards processing and a shift to global ISO standards to better support international growth. This conversation is part of a larger series featuring all seven board members, with two interviews already live on YouTube, offering a closer look at the people guiding POTA forward.

Join the conversation and subscribe to Q5 Worldwide Ham Radio.

Special thanks to DX Engineering for supporting operators across every corner of the hobby, from Parks on the Air activators to contesters and DXers pushing their stations to the limit. Their continued backing helps keep the spirit of operating alive worldwide.

snippet.title: Gobierno estadounidense autoriza negociaciones para reestructurar la deuda venezolana
snippet.description:
Nota informativa publicada por @finanzasdigital señala que el Departamento del Tesoro de Estados Unidos, @treasurydept, a través de la Oficina de Control de Activos Extranjeros (OFAC), emitió la Licencia General 58, que permite a empresas y profesionales estadounidenses prestar servicios legales, financieros y de consultoría al Gobierno de Venezuela en el marco de una eventual reestructuración de deuda. La autorización incluye a Petróleos de Venezuela (Pdvsa) y a las entidades donde la estatal posee 50% o más de participación.
La medida habilita “todas las transacciones… necesarias para la provisión de servicios legales, financieros y de consultoría… en conexión con potenciales procesos de reestructuración de deuda”. Esto abarca la evaluación, preparación y desarrollo de propuestas o materiales técnicos relacionados con una posible negociación futura.
La licencia no permite ejecutar la reestructuración ni negociar directamente con acreedores. El documento aclara que no están autorizadas “la reestructuración, transferencia o liquidación de deuda… ni negociaciones directas entre el Gobierno de Venezuela y los acreedores”. Se trata únicamente de actividades preparatorias.
Tampoco se permiten acuerdos de pago que no sean comercialmente razonables, ni operaciones denominadas en monedas digitales emitidas por el Gobierno venezolano, como el Petro. La licencia excluye además cualquier transacción que involucre a personas o entidades ubicadas en Rusia, Irán, Corea del Norte, Cuba o China, así como a individuos incluidos en la lista de Nacionales Especialmente Designados (SDN), con excepción de Pdvsa y sus filiales.

Suscríbete a nuestro canal (con la campanita), indícanos que te gusta la información compartida (Likes). ¡Solicita tus Promorreportajes!, ¡Síguenos! y recuerda:

Suscríbete a nuestro canal (con la campanita), indícanos que te gusta la información compartida (Likes). ¡Solicita tus Promorreportajes!, ¡Síguenos! y recuerda: 
¡Somos Comunicación Productiva! Contacto: informa2ve@gmail.com

snippet.description:

Antenna & Ground: Ye ek bohot lamba wire hota hai jo hawa se AM radio signals (Free Energy) ko catch karke circuit mein bhejta hai.
Tuned Circuit (Coil + Capacitor): Variable capacitor ke knob ko ghuma kar hum us exact frequency ko pakadte hain jahan hamara favorite radio station chal raha ho.
1N34A Detector (Diode): Ye is radio ka "Brain" hai! Ye high-frequency radio signal mein se aawaz (audio) ko alag (rectify) karta hai.
Headphones: Kyunki signal bohot weak hota hai, isliye aawaz ko sunne ke liye high-power wale headphones ka use kiya jata hai bina kisi amplifier ke!
⚙️ Tech Reality Check (Pro Tip): Dosto, is magic radio ko banane ke liye aapko ek chiz ka dhyan rakhna hoga—is circuit mein galti se bhi normal Silicon diode mat lagana, sirf Germanium Diode (1N34A ya Galena Crystal) hi use karna warna aawaz bilkul nahi aayegi! snippet.title: OARC Talks - Everything on the Air: An Introduction to Operating in the Great Outdoors
snippet.description:
Presenter: Ian Renton M0TRT

If you're curious about getting started in portable operation, how to go about it, where to go, or even why you should try it at all, this talk is for you. Ian only got started working portable in 2024, but quickly caught the bug, and 100-or-so park activations later, he'll share what he's learned about rigs, antennas, batteries, and operating techniques. From tough mountain climbs to the park down the road, and even just operating from your car, portable operating has something for every level of fitness and appetite for adventure!

Join OARC: https://www.oarc.uk

snippet.title: Move Over POTA! Why You Need to Try Towers On The Air (TOTA)
snippet.description:
Taking Your Signal to New Heights: An Introduction to Towers On The Air (TOTA)

If you are an amateur radio operator who loves the thrill of packing up a rig and heading into the great outdoors, you are likely already familiar with the massively popular Parks On The Air (POTA) or Summits On The Air (SOTA) programs. But what if you’re looking to add a new twist to your portable operations, chase different references, or simply take advantage of some incredible VHF/UHF elevation?

Enter Towers On The Air (TOTA).

What is Towers On The Air?

Towers On The Air (TOTA) is an international amateur radio program dedicated to the activation of lookout towers, observation decks, and viewpoints. Originally founded in the Czech Republic, the project has rapidly crossed borders, building a unified, worldwide database of towers and connecting activators and hunters across the global ham community.

The core principle of TOTA is simple: pack your portable radio gear, hike or travel to a designated lookout tower, and make contacts (QSOs) from a unique, elevated location.

How Does it Work?

Much like POTA and SOTA, the TOTA ecosystem is divided into two main roles:

Activators: Operators who go out into the field, set up their stations at or on the lookout towers, and call CQ.

Hunters: Operators (base stations, mobiles, or other portable stations) who try to make contact with the Activators from anywhere in the world.

TOTA features an automated award system. By creating an account on the TOTA website, both Activators and Hunters can log their contacts, track their statistics, and automatically earn digital diplomas based on the points they collect. The platform provides interactive maps, historical and technical data about the towers, and photos to help you plan your next activation.

TOTA vs. POTA: A Perfect Match

You don't have to choose between TOTA and POTA; in fact, they are incredibly complementary programs. Here is how TOTA differs from POTA and why it makes a great addition to your portable repertoire:

1. The Power of Elevation

While a POTA reference simply requires you to be within the administrative boundary of a state or national park (which could be deep in a valley or dense forest), a TOTA reference is inherently elevated. Lookout towers are built to see over the surrounding terrain. This offers a massive tactical advantage for radio operators. While HF will work great, the high, clear line-of-sight makes TOTA absolutely spectacular for VHF/UHF simplex operations.

2. The "Two-Fer" Opportunity

Because lookout towers are frequently located inside state and national parks, you can often activate both a POTA reference and a TOTA reference simultaneously. By mentioning both references in your CQ call or spotting networks, you instantly double your pileup, attracting hunters from both communities!

3. Different Footprints and Logistics

POTA allows you to operate from the comfort of a picnic table, the tailgate of your truck, or even inside your car. TOTA encourages a different kind of adventure. Many towers require a hike to reach and have limited space at the top. This challenges you to rethink your portable setup: you might need to leave the heavy battery and bulky antenna behind in favor of an ultra-lightweight QRP rig and a compact vertical or wire that can be safely deployed from an observation deck.

4. Tourism and Family-Friendly

Towers are natural tourist attractions. If you struggle to convince your family to sit in a random park for three hours while you play radio, TOTA offers a great compromise. You get to activate a radio reference, and your family gets to enjoy a hike, historic architecture, and breathtaking panoramic views.

How to Get Started

TOTA is fully compatible with other outdoor radio programs and is built on the spirit of community and exploration.

Visit the Website: Head over to https://www.wwtota.com.

Register: Create an account using your valid amateur radio callsign.

Find a Tower: Use the interactive map to locate a TOTA reference near you. The database currently includes towers in the Czech Republic, Slovakia, Germany, Austria, Poland, and is actively expanding to the Netherlands, Puerto Rico, the Dominican Republic, and beyond.

Hit the Airwaves: Pack your radio, enjoy the view, and get on the air!

Whether you are a seasoned POTA veteran looking for a fresh challenge, or a new ham wanting to combine a weekend hike with some radio time, Towers On The Air offers a fantastic way to elevate your portable operating experience.

snippet.title: Okeechobee POTA with K8MRD Did NOT Go As Planned!!
snippet.description:
You win some, you lose some!! I barely activated, and I think I may have gotten the very last of the decent bands for the day. Unfortunately, Mike didn't get that lucky. 

We tried our best and had an absolute blast, which is really what matters the most, so not a wasted day at all!! The IC-7300 performed like a champ, and I got to use my new PackTenna!! Honestly, if we'd had more time, we would have sat there another hour to get it done. What a gorgeous park!!

We live to POTA another day. Keep an eye out for Mike's (K8MRD) next video on @hamradiotube, there may be a redemption video on its way. 😂

Thank you for watching!! If you're new to my channel, please feel free to hit that like and subscribe, and click that little bell so you don't miss the next video. 

See you all soon!!

73
K8FAE

snippet.title: Solar Cycle 25 Ending? What Every POTA Op Needs To Do NOW
snippet.description:
If you've turned on your HF rig recently, you might have noticed a shift in the airwaves. The effortless, wide-open bands we enjoyed just a short time ago are becoming a bit more temperamental. In a recent video discussion, I sat down with Greg (KJ6ER)—renowned antenna designer and mastermind behind popular portable antennas like the POTA PERformer and the Marauder—to talk about the current state of amateur radio.

The reality is clear: Solar Cycle 25 reached its apex roughly 6 to 18 months ago (peaking in late 2024), and we are now entering a period of decline. With propagation conditions expected to bottom out around late 2030, the "easy days" of casual operating are slowly fading. But for portable operators willing to adapt, the hobby is about to get a lot more rewarding.

Here is how you can pivot your operating strategy to thrive as Solar Cycle 25 winds down.

From Casual Trolling to "Earned DX"

At the peak of a solar cycle, even a compromised setup can net global contacts. You could simply spin the dial—casual "trolling"—and stumble into a massive pileup. Today, that mindset needs to shift.

As we slide down the backside of Cycle 25, operators must embrace the concept of "Earned DX." This means you can no longer rely on hyper-charged ionospheric conditions to do the heavy lifting. Instead, successful contacts will require deliberate precision. You must be strategic about the band you choose, the mode you operate in, and the exact time you press the PTT button.

Tactical Band and Mode Selection

As solar flux indices drop, the higher frequency bands (like 10, 12, and 15 meters) will become less reliable and open less frequently. To keep your logbook full, you need to adapt your hunting grounds:

The New Workhorses: The 20-meter and 40-meter bands are stepping back into their traditional roles as the heavy lifters of HF radio. Expect to spend the majority of your operating time here.

The Digital & CW Refugees: As voice (SSB) contacts become more difficult to pull out of the noise floor, bands like 60-meter, 80-meter, and 30-meter will become vital.

Mode Adaptation: If you have been strictly a voice operator, now is the time to branch out. Continuous Wave (CW) and digital modes (like FT8, FT4, and PSK) are inherently more efficient than Sideband. When the bands are crowded or signals are weak, these modes can cut through the atmospheric noise and secure contacts that would be impossible on voice.

Maximizing the Clock: Gray Line and Contests

Timing is everything in a declining solar cycle. To maximize your reach, you must use the Earth's rotation to your advantage:

Catching the Gray Line: The "gray line" is the band of twilight separating day and night on Earth. Operating during your local sunrise or sunset can create highly efficient propagation paths, temporarily opening up impressive DX opportunities on lower bands that might otherwise seem dead.

Leveraging Contest Weekends: During solar lulls, bands can seem deceptively quiet simply because fewer people are calling CQ. Contest weekends solve this by injecting thousands of active operators into the spectrum. Even if you aren't a serious contester, these weekends are golden opportunities to find activity and test your setup.

The Hardware: Efficiency is King

Greg (KJ6ER) is a master of antenna design, so it’s no surprise that he emphasizes one critical technical truth: Radiation efficiency is the most critical factor for success.

When propagation is poor, you cannot afford to waste your signal in a lossy matching network or an inefficient radiating element. Adding a linear amplifier to a poor antenna just makes you a louder bad signal. Instead, the focus must be on high-efficiency, resonant systems. Antennas like Greg’s PERformer (a highly efficient elevated vertical) or the Marauder (a 2-element Moxon-inspired directional array) give portable operators a massive advantage by focusing their limited power exactly where it needs to go.

Advanced Techniques to Explore:

Dedicated Receive Antennas: On the lower bands (40m, 80m, 160m), atmospheric noise can bury weak signals. Experimenting with dedicated receive antennas, such as Beverages or Beverage-on-Ground (BOG) wires, can drastically drop your noise floor.

Long-Path Propagation: When the direct, short-path route to a DX station is closed by the ionosphere, point your antenna the opposite way. Long-path propagation can sometimes shoot your signal the long way around the globe, bypassing dead zones.

The Operator's Digital Toolkit

You can no longer guess what the sun is doing; you need data. Incorporate real-time propagation tools into your daily routine. Resources like DXView, SolarHam, and HamDash are essential for monitoring solar weather, checking active spotting clusters, and predicting where your signal can actually travel before you even unpack your radio.

Get out there, tune your antennas, and start earning your DX.

snippet.title: Quick SOTA Doubler: 2 Easy Summits, 1 Trip
snippet.description:
Quick SOTA Doubler: 2 Easy Summits, 1 Trip | McElroy Hill & Beckwourth Peak (W6/NS-316 & W6/NS-228)

Looking for a fast and rewarding SOTA activation day? In this video, I take on a two-summit adventure just about an hour from Reno, Nevada—activating McElroy Hill (W6/NS-316) and Beckwourth Peak (W6/NS-228) in a single outing.

The first hike is short but steep—less than 0.5 miles with ~580 ft of elevation gain—while the second stretches out to 1.25 miles with 766 ft of gain. With only a 20-minute drive between trailheads, this makes for a perfect SOTA “doubler” day that balances effort and fun.

Operating 100% CW, I set up a lightweight portable station featuring:
•Elecraft KX2 (QRP HF transceiver)
•40m End-Fed Half Wave (EFHW) antenna with homebrew UNUN
•Spiderbeam 7m telescopic mast

You’ll hear real Morse code QSOs, see summit conditions, and get a feel for what it’s like to combine ham radio, hiking, and scenic Northern California views near Sierraville and Portola.

Whether you're into SOTA, portable QRP ops, CW operating, or just enjoy getting outdoors, this is a great example of how to maximize a day in the field.

📍 Summits:
McElroy Hill – W6/NS-316
Beckwourth Peak – W6/NS-228

📡 Location: Near Sierraville & Portola, California (about 1 hour from Reno, NV)

💬 Let me know in the comments:
Would you tackle both summits in one trip, or take them separately?

👍 If you enjoy SOTA and CW content, consider liking and subscribing for more portable ops adventures.

#SOTA #HamRadio #QRP #CW #PortableOps #ElecraftKX2 #SummitsOnTheAir #AmateurRadio #Backpacking #HikingCalifornia #EFHW #MorseCode #AE7I

snippet.title: I may have found the “20m MAGIC ANTENNA! Against all odds, great DX!
snippet.description:
I never let "bad band conditions" keep me from going out to a park and activating, but this was almost that day.  Every one and every thing I had access to suggested the bands were so bad it wasn't even worth going out.  
I went anyway, and I'm so glad I did.  I deployed my "Picnic POTA" antenna setup with 2 elevated radial 180 degrees opposed to one another.  I was stunned!  In spite of all the information I had and in spite of the comments I received from Hunters about how bad things were, I killed it.  The antenna setup was amazing and I think I may have found the 20m Magic Antenna.  111 contacts in barely over an hour including DX to Russis, Spain, Germany, Portugal, and Puerto Rico.
As I often say, just go out and call CQ.  Good things are bound to happen.
Come along as we look at the antenna setup that defied all the odds.

snippet.title: 29 Years as a Technician… Finally Upgraded to General | Rollin KF4KZA
snippet.description:
After spending nearly 29 years as a Technician — and being away from ham radio for about 25 of those years — Rollin KF4KZA decided it was time to get back on the air and finally upgrade to his General license.

Although he had passed the General written exam years ago, he never completed the code requirement at the time. Wanting to make sure he was fully prepared to pass the modern General exam, he decided to use Ham Radio Prep.

What stood out most?

📚 The course was broken down into small, manageable lessons
📝 The written study guides highlighted the most important information
🎥 The structure made reviewing and retaining material much easier

Instead of feeling overwhelmed by a large amount of information, Rollin was able to move through the material step by step — and completed the course in about two weeks.

For anyone returning to ham radio after years away, this testimonial is proof that getting back into the hobby — and upgrading your license — is absolutely achievable.

Thanks again to Rollin G. for sharing his experience with Ham Radio Prep 📡

snippet.title: Drake Dominated Ham Radio for 20 Years — Then Made One Mistake
snippet.description:
The R. L. Drake Company was once the most respected name in American amateur radio. For over twenty years, their receivers and transceivers were the equipment that serious operators swore by. The blue glow of a Drake dial was one of the most recognized symbols in the hobby. Before any of that, their engineers built electronic warfare equipment used during the Second World War, working out of a building so small they shared it with a coat hanger manufacturer.

Robert Lloyd Drake started the company in 1943 with a handful of employees and a specialization in electronic filtering. What he built during the war earned him military contracts and a reputation for precision. After the war, he kept the company alive through years of unglamorous contract work before developing a receiver that bet against everything the rest of the industry was doing. That bet paid off and turned Drake into a global brand.

But the same philosophy that made them dominant eventually became the thing their competitors used against them. This video covers the full arc, from a rented workshop in Dayton, Ohio to a bankruptcy filing eight decades later.

snippet.title: My Garage Door is Now Smarter Than My Car (UniFi Access Install)
snippet.description:
In this video, I install UniFi Access on my garage door using a Gate Hub and G3 Flex keypad, creating a fully functional smart garage system with PIN access.

But I took it a step further...

Unlike most setups, this system actually knows the real position of the garage door (open or closed) and reports it correctly inside the UniFi app — no guessing, no false states.

As a bonus, I repurposed a G6 Bullet camera to automatically read my license plate and trigger the garage door to open when I pull in.

In this video I cover:
- Full UniFi Access garage door wiring and setup
- Using a Gate Hub for garage integration
- Adding keypad PIN access
- Configuring real door position status
- License plate recognition automation with UniFi Protect

Let me know what you'd improve or automate next.
